rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes for interface behavior; use implementation scopes for assignment bindings.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain all AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Refer to component ports only through valid variables or outputs in type-level contracts.
Define constants and equations in component type annexes when describing interface behavior.
Use visible identifiers from the component's feature set or declared constants in AGREE expressions.
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties directly in AGREE unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses `assume "label": expression;`, `guarantee "label": expression;`, `eq name : type = expression;`, and `const name : type = value;`.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-specific assignments in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens directly in AGREE expressions.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their automatic scoping in AGREE
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
